Two standard architectures: (A) standalone Next.js repo for complex needs, (B) paragu-ai-builder tenant for simpler needs. This skill covers Architecture A — standalone Next.js.", "tags": ["website", "client", "professional-services", "advisory", "nextjs", "docker", "swarn", "golden-visa", "paraguay", "b2b"], "related_skills": ["paragu-ai-builder", "py-market-research"], "content": "--- name: professional-services-website description: >- Scaffold, deploy, and iterate on professional-services client websites: consultancies, law firms, advisory firms, investment advisors, B2B suppliers. Two standard architectures: (A) standalone Next.js repo for complex needs, (B) paragu-ai-builder tenant for simpler needs. This skill covers Architecture A — standalone Next.js. tags: - website - client - professional-services - advisory - nextjs - docker - swarn - golden-visa - paraguay - b2b related_skills: - paragu-ai-builder - py-market-research references: - golden-visa-mvp-pattern.md - b2b-site-from-template.md - pitchy-blindex-worked-example.md - paragu-ai-builder-tenant-analysis.md - docker-build-with-file-deps.md - client-market-intelligence-analysis.md - client-business-assessment-template.md triggers: - golden visa - advisory website - consultancy website - professional services site - dual audience website - law firm site - investment advisor website - build a site for a consultancy - b2b lead generation site - construction website - industrial supplier website - glass supplier website - b2b product catalog site - quote request website - template to b2b - b2b from template - b2b lead generation site - construction company website - glass supplier website - industrial website - pages router site - standalone nextjs site --- # Professional-Services Website (Architecture A) Build a standalone Next.js 15 site for a professional services firm (consultancy, law firm, advisory, B2B supplier) with dual-audience routing or single-audience lead gen. **Two distinct sub-types:** | Sub-type | Example | Content Pattern | CTA Pattern | |----------|---------|----------------|-------------| | **B2C Advisory** | Golden Visa, Law Firm | Dual-path (investor/business), testimonials, comparison tables | Book a call, contact form | | **B2B Lead Gen** | Glass supplier, industrial products | Technical specs, project portfolio, certifications | "Request quote" → WhatsApp, quote form | ## When to Use This vs the Builder | Criteria | Use Standalone (Arch A) | Use Builder (Arch B) | |---|---|---| | Dual-path routing (investor vs business) | ✅ | ❌ (single flow) | | Age-gate / path-choice modal | ✅ | ❌ | | Complex comparison tables | ✅ | Limited | | FAQ with multi-tab switching | ✅ | Limited | | 5+ custom pages | ✅ | ❌ (builder does 1 page) | | Team has 3+ staff profiles | ✅ | ✅ | | Simple brochure (home + contact) | ❌ | ✅ | | B2B with technical product catalog | ✅ | ❌ | | Quote request form | ✅ | ❌ | | No prices (custom quoting) | ✅ N/A | ✅ N/A | --- ## Sub-type A: B2C Advisory (Golden Visa Pattern) See the original implementation in `references/golden-visa-mvp-pattern.md`. Key traits: - Entry modal (language + path selection) - Investor and Business landing paths - Testimonials, trust signals, team profiles - Dark theme with gold accents --- ## Sub-type B: B2B Lead Gen (Pitchy Pattern) Build a B2B site from the `Ai-Whisperers/template-nextjs-client` template. This template is e-commerce B2C by default — you must transform it. ### Transformation Checklist (Remove B2C, Add B2B) **REMOVE these from the cloned template:** - `app/tienda/` — product store with cart - `app/envio/` — shipping info - `app/promociones/` — promotions - `app/producto/[slug]/` — individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) - `components/search-autocomplete.tsx` — no product search needed - `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx` — B2B stays light theme - `components/payment-methods.tsx` — no payments - `components/quick-order.tsx` — no quick ordering - `components/recently-viewed.tsx` — no recently viewed - `components/product-card.tsx` — replace with B2B product card (no price) - `components/hero-carousel.tsx` — single hero image for B2B - `components/promo-carousel.tsx` — no promotions - `components/bottom-nav.tsx` — not needed - `lib/wishlist.ts` and `lib/recently-viewed.ts` — not needed - `lib/hooks/use-wishlist.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-recently-viewed.ts` — remove, update `lib/hooks/index.ts` - `public/images/products/` and `public/images/categories/` — template placeholder images - `public/manifest.json`, `public/sw.js` — PWA files from template **CREATE these new pages:** - `app/productos/page.tsx` — Product catalog with specs, no prices, "Request quote" per item - `app/proyectos/page.tsx` — Portfolio gallery - `app/servicios/page.tsx` — Service listing with process steps - `app/cotizar/page.tsx` — Quote request form → WhatsApp ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Mapping The template pins `^0.1.0` but the GitHub Packages registry has newer versions. When `npm install` fails with `ETARGET` or `401`, check and set exact versions: ```bash # Check available versions (requires GITHUB_TOKEN in env) GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) npm view @ai-whisperers/admin version ``` | Package | Pinned | Actual latest (as of May 2026) | |---------|--------|-------------------------------| | `@ai-whisperers/ui` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.4.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/auth` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/admin` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.1` | | `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/seo` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | Remove `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest` from devDependencies — it doesn't exist in the registry. ### Content Structure for Blog The template's blog components expect `blog.posts[]` (array under a posts key). If your content JSON has `blog` as a flat array, you must adapt the components: ```tsx // In app/blog/page.tsx — adapt data structure const b = { posts: (c.blog || []), categories: [...new Set((c.blog || []).map((p: any) => p.category).filter(Boolean))], title: "Blog", subtitle: "" } // In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx const post = ((c.blog || []) as any[]).find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) ``` ### B2B Theme Config Use a light theme with blue/cyan palette for construction/industrial B2B: ```json { "palettes": { "default": { "primary": "#0F62FE", "primaryForeground": "#FFFFFF", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "secondaryForeground": "#E2E8F0", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA", "surfaceLight": "#E8ECF0", "text": "#1A1A2E", "textLight": "#4A5568", "textMuted": "#6B7280" } }, "defaultPalette": "default", "theme": "light" } ``` ### SEO Strategy for Zero-Competition Keywords For B2B industrial/construction sites in Paraguay, search for keywords with ZERO existing competition. The market analysis repo has these patterns: ```bash # Find keywords with no competing pages "muro cortina Paraguay" → no rankable results "vidrio fachada Asunción" → no rankable results "vidrio templado Paraguay" → low competition ``` Write 3 blog posts targeting these before launch. Post schedule: one per day for the first 3 days. ### Standard B2B Pages | Page | Purpose | Content | |------|---------|---------| | `/` | Hero + features + product overview + CTA | B2B value props, trust signals | | `/productos` | Technical product catalog | Specs, applications, no prices, "Request quote" CTA per item | | `/proyectos` | Portfolio gallery | Project photos, scope, client names | | `/servicios` | Service listing | Process steps, what each service includes | | `/cotizar` | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product selection, m2, timeline → WhatsApp | | `/nosotros` | Company story | Stats, team, certifications, values | | `/blog` | SEO content | Technical articles, buyer education | | `/contacto` | Contact | Form + phone + WhatsApp + map | | `/faq` | FAQ | Common customer questions | --- ## Sub-type C: Pages Router Standalone (Nexa Paraguay Pattern) A Pages Router Next.js site deployed as a standalone Docker image, with a single locale (ES) and content-driven sections. Images are referenced in `images.json` and resolved at runtime. Used when the client needs a lighter-weight site than the full App Router architecture. ### Architecture ``` repo-root/ ├── src/pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # homepage — getServerSideProps loads content + images │ ├── [slug].tsx # all other pages — SLUG_MAP routes slugs to page configs │ └── admin/content.tsx # optional CMS page ├── src/components/ │ ├── sections.tsx # SECTION_MAP with HeroSection, ProgramsSection, etc. │ ├── content.ts # resolveContent(), resolveImage() helpers │ ├── Header.tsx # renders logo + nav │ └── Footer.tsx ├── nexa-pages/ # page config JSON files (home.json, servicios.json, etc.) ├── content/ │ └── es.json # all site content (single locale) ├── images.json # image manifest — all image paths resolved through this ├── public/images/ # actual image files (brand/, hero/, programs/, etc.) ├── Dockerfile # must include images.json copying at runtime stage ├── docker-compose.yml # Traefik labels at service-level, NOT deploy: labels └── package.json # may use file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/... local deps ``` ### Image Resolution Pattern Content uses `"@src:trust.certificate"` or `"$img": "programs.tierBase"` syntax referencing keys in `images.json`. A helper resolves these at runtime: ```typescript // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.startsWith('@img:') ? ref.replace('@img:', '') : ref.replace('@src:', '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) { if (obj?.[p]) obj = obj[p] else return '' } return obj?.src || obj?.fallbackSrc || '' } return ref } ``` ### Critical: images.json Must Be in Docker Image The Dockerfile does NOT copy the entire repo — only specific dirs. You MUST add `images.json` explicitly: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/images.json ./images.json # ← REQUIRED COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./nexa-pages ``` Without this line, `loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')` returns null at runtime and every image reference resolves to empty string — no images render, no error message. ### Passing Images to Section Components Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must: 1. Load `images.json` in `getServerSideProps`: ```typescript const images = loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')?.images || {} ``` 2. Pass it to the return object: ```typescript return { props: { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, timestamp: Date.now() } } ``` 3. Pass it to section components AND reconstruct pageContent properly: ```tsx if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` **Why this matters**: Old `sections.tsx` components access data via `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.trust` — they expect the key name to be the LAST segment of the content key (`home.hero` → `hero`). Without this reconstruction using `.split('.').pop()`, the pageContent would use `section.id` (e.g. `"stats-counter"`) which doesn't match what the component looks for (`"stats"`), and ALL section data would be invisible despite the component rendering. **This must be applied identically in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` (they are near-duplicates).** If `package.json` has `"@ai-whisperers/admin": "file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/..."`, the Docker build fails because the `file:` path doesn't exist inside the container. **Fix:** Build on the VPS host first (`npm install && npm run build`), then build Docker from the already-ready `node_modules`. The `COPY . .` in the Dockerfile picks up the existing `node_modules` directory, and `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` runs against the already-installed deps. This works because npm skips re-installation for packages it can't resolve via `file:` when they're already in `node_modules`. ### SVG Logo Fallback When the client has no designer-provided SVG logo, create one inline: ```svg ``` Create three variants: - `logo.svg` — standard (navy on white background) - `logo-dark.svg` — white text for dark backgrounds (footer) - `logo-icon.svg` — icon-only mark Reference in `images.json`: ```json "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "srcSvg": "/images/brand/logo.svg", ... } ``` Update `Header.tsx` to render `` instead of plain text `{businessName}`. ### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone For a complete deploy checklist covering ALL items in this section plus verification steps, see `references/client-site-launch-checklist.md`. For the section component building pattern (FAQ, Blog, Team, Privacy, Glossary, etc.), see `references/section-components-pattern.md`. For theme standardisation and design-token extraction, see the `src/theme.ts` pattern described below. For a comprehensive code-quality audit checklist covering the 40+ issues found in a production Pages Router site, see `references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md`. - **Theme standardisation**: After building section components, extract all hardcoded hex colors, border-radius values, and spacing into a single `src/theme.ts` file: ```typescript export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', text: '#444', muted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0' }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' }, fonts: { body: "'Inter', sans-serif", heading: "'Playfair Display', serif" }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` Import in components: `import { theme } from '../theme'` then `style={{ background: theme.colors.primary }}`. Destructure frequently-used keys at top of file: `const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing`. - **Mobile navigation**: The hamburger button in Header.tsx is often hidden (`display: none`) in the initial scaffold. Enable it with a `@media (max-width: 768px)` CSS-in-JS block that toggles nav visibility via React state: ```tsx ``` - **Footer social links**: site.json typically has `social: { linkedin, instagram }` but the Footer component doesn't render them. Add `socialLinks` to the footer content object and render as `` tags with the site.json social URLs. - **Cache headers for SSR pages**: `getServerSideProps` reads JSON from disk on every request. Add caching via `res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300')` or return a `headers` key from `getServerSideProps` (Next.js 13+ supports returning `headers` from `getServerSideProps`). - **Navigation hrefs**: The content may use multi-tenant paths like `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. For standalone deployment, replace with plain paths: `/programas`. Check ALL occurrences in ALL content JSON files (es, en, nl, de) — nav item hrefs, hero CTA links, hero CTA secondary links, blog-related URLs, and ANY href field. A missed multiclient path in content/en.json nav hrefs causes EVERY nav link in the EN locale to 404. The Header component must prepend the locale prefix to nav hrefs (e.g., `/servicios` → `/en/servicios`) after stripping any remaining multiclient prefix. Use a grep across all locale files: `grep -rn '/s/' content/*.json`. - **Placeholder template variables**: Content may have `{{taxRate}}` placeholders. If the component doesn't replace them, pre-replace in the content JSON with the actual value. Search across ALL pages content, not just home. - **Blog post links must include locale prefix**: Blog "Leer más" / "Read more" links in the BlogSection component must prepend the current locale. Without this, blog links on EN/NL/DE pages navigate to `/blog/post-slug` (no locale) which hits the catch-all route without the locale prefix, causing a 404 or wrong-content render. Fix: use `useRouter` from `next/router` and prepend: `href={\`/${locale}/blog/${post.slug}\`}`. The locale variable comes from the page component's `locale` prop, NOT from `router.locale` (which may be undefined in Pages Router). - **Content JSON corruption from patch tool**: The `patch` tool can corrupt large JSON files (208KB, 900+ lines) by misplacing commas. A missing comma between `}` and `\"seo\"` breaks the entire site — every page SSR returns `content: {}` with zero Docker errors. **Always validate after every JSON edit**: `python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')\"`. For safety, prefer Python manipulation: `json.load()` + `json.dump()` instead of `patch` or `sed` on large JSON files. Add pre-deploy validation: `for f in content/*.json; do python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('$f'))\" && echo \"$f: VALID\" || exit 1; done`. - **Docker service labels at service-level**: In `docker-compose.yml`, Traefik labels must be at `services.web.deploy.labels` level, NOT at `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone ignores `deploy:` labels. - **Content items use q/a keys**: FAQ, privacy, and glossary items in content JSON may use `q`/`a` keys instead of `pregunta`/`respuesta` or `question`/`answer` or `term`/`definition`. Support ALL variants in component logic: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`. Prefer `q`/`a` first since that's the most common format in the content source. - **Hero CTA hrefs share the same problem**: Nav hrefs aren't the only place multiclient paths hide. Hero CTAs (`home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref`, `home.hero.ctaSecondaryHref`) in EN/NL/DE locale files also use `/s/en/nexa-paraguay/` paths. These cause the hero buttons on non-ES locales to 404. Run a systematic grep: `grep -rn 'cta.*Href' content/*.json | grep '/s/'`. - **Section components receive resolved data directly**: When dispatching to SECTION_MAP, pass `resolveContent(content, section.content)` as `pageContent`, NOT the full page build result. Otherwise the component needs to dig through intermediary keys. Accept both `pageContent` (direct data) and `data` (backward-compat) in component props. - **SECTION_MAP must be in both index.tsx and [slug].tsx**: Both files dispatch section components. Always add new entries to both. - **Rich fallback for [slug].tsx**: The generic section renderer in `[slug].tsx` must handle more than just `headline`/`subheadline`. Add eyebrow text, body content paragraphs, and item grids (for FAQ, glossary, services, blog posts, team members). The renderer should detect `data.items`, `data.groups`, `data.pillars` arrays and render them as card grids. Each item card shows `title`/`pregunta`/`question`/`term` + `description`/`respuesta`/`answer`/`definition`/`body`. - **buildPageContent removal breaks section rendering**: Removing `buildPageContent` is recommended (it's redundant once the dispatch passes sectionData directly), BUT you must reconstruct `pageContent` with the correct key name. Section components in `sections.tsx` access data via `pageContent.hero` — they expect the content key's LAST segment (`home.hero` → `hero`), NOT the section id (`hero` happens to match but `stats-counter` doesn't match `stats`). The dispatch must use `section.content?.split('.').pop()` as the pageContent key. Without this, ALL section data appears invisible despite the component rendering — the hero background image won't load, trust/programs/process images won't appear, and stats won't show values. See the "Passing Images to Section Components" section above for the correct dispatch code. - **Every page config needs SLUG_MAP entry**: If a new page config JSON is added to `nexa-pages/`, it MUST also be added to `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it will 404. The `SLUG_MAP` is the routing layer between URL slugs and page config filenames. - **image paths in images.json**: After copying `images.json` from the paragu-ai-builder source, ALL paths use `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/...` prefix which only works in the multi-tenant context. For standalone deployment, batch-replace to `/images/...`. Use a Python script to walk the JSON and fix all `src`, `fallbackSrc`, `fallback`, and `basePath` fields. - **Image path verification**: After fixing paths, verify every referenced image exists on disk with a script. The `images.json` may reference images that don't exist yet. Run `python3 -c "..."` to compare referenced src paths against actual files in `public/images/`. ## Deployment (Both Sub-types) ```yaml # docker-compose.yml services: web: build: . image: :prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers..entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers..rule=Host(`.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers..tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers..tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services..loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` **Important:** The template's docker-compose.yml will have hardcoded old values (site name, image name, Traefik rules). Update ALL of them for the new site. ### CI/CD Registration After pushing the repo, add it to ci-cd's `clients.json`: ```json "": { "site": "", "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/", "url": "https://.paragu-ai.com", "type": "nextjs", "pm": "npm", "node": "20", "docker_image": ":prod", "docker_service": "_web", "deploy_strategy": "build-and-scp" } ``` ### DNS (Cloudflare) Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard for paragu-ai.com: - Type: A - Name: `` - Content: `72.61.44.159` - Proxy: Proxied No Cloudflare API key is available in this environment — DNS changes must be done through the Cloudflare dashboard. ## Project Structure (Common) ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # providers + metadata + fonts │ ├── page.tsx # home page │ ├── productos/ # (B2B) product catalog │ ├── proyectos/ # (B2B) portfolio │ ├── servicios/ # (B2B) services │ ├── cotizar/ # (B2B) quote form │ ├── blog/ # blog index + [slug] │ ├── nosotros/ # about page │ ├── contacto/ # contact form │ └── faq/ # FAQ accordion ├── components/ # reusable UI components ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # all site content │ └── tokens.json # design tokens ├── Dockerfile ├── docker-compose.yml └── package.json ``` ## Content Management All site copy lives in `content/es.json` (single file). Structure per site type: ### B2B content sections ```json { "home": { "hero": {}, "features": {}, "finalCta": {} }, "products": [{ id, name, description, features[], applications[], cta }], "proyectos": [{ name, location, scope, image }], "servicios": [{ id, title, description, icon }], "nosotros": { story, stats[], values[] }, "faq": [{ question, answer }], "blog": [{ slug, title, excerpt, content }] } ``` ## Multi-Locale Routing (Activation Pattern) All four locales (`es`, `en`, `nl`, `de`) should be activated as infrastructure, not just the default. The pattern: ### Middleware Locale Detection **CRITICAL: Cookie name must match getServerSideProps.** The middleware reads `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie, and getServerSideProps sets it as `NEXT_LOCALE`. If they disagree, Accept-Language header overrides on every visit. Also check `request.cookies.get('locale')` for backward compatibility with any previous middleware version. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json. // src/middleware.ts const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'es' export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const { pathname } = request.nextUrl const firstSegment = pathname.split('/')[1] const hasLocale = LOCALES.includes(firstSegment) if (!hasLocale && !pathname.startsWith('/_next') && !pathname.startsWith('/images') && pathname !== '/favicon.svg') { // Read NEXT_LOCALE first (set by getServerSideProps), fall back to 'locale' (legacy) let preferred = request.cookies.get('NEXT_LOCALE')?.value || request.cookies.get('locale')?.value || DEFAULT_LOCALE if (!LOCALES.includes(preferred)) { const acceptLang = request.headers.get('accept-language') || '' for (const loc of LOCALES) { if (acceptLang.startsWith(loc) || acceptLang.includes(` ${loc}`) || acceptLang.includes(`;${loc}`)) { preferred = loc; break } } } const url = new URL(`/${preferred}${pathname}`, request.url) if (request.nextUrl.search) url.search = request.nextUrl.search return NextResponse.redirect(url, 302) } const result = NextResponse.next() return result } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|images|favicon\\.svg).*)'], } ``` ### Locale Detection in getServerSideProps — URL Prefix MUST Win **This is the most common multi-locale bug.** The URL prefix (`/es/servicios`) must ALWAYS override cookie and Accept-Language. Without tracking whether the URL explicitly specified a locale, a stale `NEXT_LOCALE=en` cookie will override `/es/`: ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params, req, res }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' let hasUrlLocale = false const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] // 1) URL prefix locale takes highest priority if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; hasUrlLocale = true slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; hasUrlLocale = true; slug = 'home' } // 2) If no URL locale, check cookie (only if URL didn't specify) if (!hasUrlLocale) { const cookieLocale = req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (cookieLocale && LOCALES.includes(cookieLocale)) { locale = cookieLocale } } // 3) If still default and no cookie, detect from Accept-Language (only if URL didn't specify) const noCookie = !req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (!hasUrlLocale && locale === 'es' && noCookie) { const detected = detectLocaleFromHeader(req?.headers?.['accept-language']) if (detected) locale = detected } // Set locale cookie for persistence const cookieMaxAge = 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', `NEXT_LOCALE=${locale}; Path=/; Max-Age=${cookieMaxAge}; SameSite=Lax`) // ... } ``` ### Header: Nav Hrefs Must Prepend Locale Prefix After stripping any multiclient prefix (`/s/en/nexa-paraguay/`), prepend the current locale to every nav href. The language switcher must also be in the header — implement a dropdown with flag emoji + language code: ```tsx // In Header.tsx const LOCALE_FLAGS = { es: '🇪🇸 ES', en: '🇬🇧 EN', nl: '🇳🇱 NL', de: '🇩🇪 DE' } const currentLocale = locale || 'es' function switchLocale(newLocale: string) { const path = router.asPath const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] let cleanPath = path const parts = path.split('/').filter(Boolean) if (parts.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { cleanPath = '/' + parts.slice(1).join('/') || '/' } window.location.href = '/' + newLocale + cleanPath } // Nav item href rendering let href = item.href || '#' // Strip existing multiclient prefix href = href.replace(/^\/s\/[^/]+\/[^/]+/, '') // Prepend locale prefix for internal links if (!href.startsWith('http') && !href.startsWith('/' + currentLocale)) { href = '/' + currentLocale + href } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Verification Write a comparison script (`scripts/compare-locale-keys.py`) that walks key paths across all locale JSONs using dot-separated paths and finds truly missing keys (not structural differences). Add empty strings for missing keys: ```python import json def walk(obj, path=''): results = set() if isinstance(obj, dict): for k, v in obj.items(): p = f'{path}.{k}' if path else k results.add(p) results |= walk(v, p) elif isinstance(obj, list) and obj and isinstance(obj[0], dict): for i, item in enumerate(obj): results |= walk(item, path) else: results.add(path) return results ``` ### Catch-All Route [[...slug]] Instead of [slug] To support `/en/faq` style multi-segment URLs, use `[[...slug]]` NOT `[slug]`: ```bash mv src/pages/"[slug].tsx" src/pages/"[[...slug]].tsx" ``` This captures `/` → empty array, `/faq` → `['faq']`, `/en/faq` → `['en', 'faq']`. `index.tsx` CANNOT coexist — delete it and handle the home page case within `[[...slug]]`. ### Locale Extraction ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } const fullContent = loadJSON(process.cwd() + '/content', `${locale}.json`) || ... } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Before activating, audit all locale JSON files against the reference (`es.json`). Walk every key and check it exists in each locale. Add missing keys with empty strings (placeholders until translated). The gaps are typically 10-15 keys per locale: new pages not propagated, recently-added content keys, object sub-keys. ### Verification ```bash for loc in es en nl de; do for page in "" faq sobre servicios programas contacto blog privacidad glosario; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://site.com/${loc}/${page}" done done ``` ## Asset Activation Policy Nothing gets deleted without client confirmation. Every dormant asset is an unactivated feature. Keep all `@ai-whisperers/*` packages installed (tree-shaken at build time, zero runtime cost). Keep unreferenced images on disk and in the manifest (pre-generated for future campaigns, <1MB total). #### Content JSON Integrity Check (Pre-Deploy) Before building the Docker image, validate ALL content JSON files. A corrupt JSON makes SSR return `content: {}` (empty pages) with zero errors in Docker logs: ```bash python3 -c " import json, os for f in os.listdir('content'): if f.endswith('.json'): try: json.load(open(f'content/{f}')) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: print(f'CORRUPT: {f} — {e}') exit(1) print('All content JSON valid') " ``` ### Stale .next/ directory pitfall After `git pull origin main` that changes content JSON files or source code, the existing `.next/` directory may be stale. The Docker `COPY . .` includes `.next/`, so stale build output + new content = empty SSR pages. Fix: ```bash rm -rf .next/ # Only when source/content files changed npm run build # Rebuild from scratch ``` After any content edit or page config change, validate all section content keys resolve: ```python import json, os with open('content/es.json') as f: content = json.load(f) for fname in os.listdir('nexa-pages'): cfg = json.load(open(f'nexa-pages/{fname}')) for sec in cfg.get('sections', []): ck = sec.get('content', sec['id']) resolved = content for part in ck.split('.'): if isinstance(resolved, dict) and part in resolved: resolved = resolved[part] else: resolved = None; break if resolved is None: print(f"MISSING: {fname} → {sec['id']} → {ck}") ``` ## Client Site Deep-Dive — Full Business Assessment When a client site already exists with docs (like Nexa Paraguay), don't just look at code — produce a structured assessment that covers ALL areas the business owner needs to see. ### Assessment Structure 1. **Code & Content Audit** — browse the live site + examine repo structure + read all docs 2. **Competitive Position** — price positioning, AI readiness, niche focus (use existing docs if they exist) 3. **Financial Model Review** — unit economics, funnel estimates, cost structure (which docs exist) 4. **Gap Analysis** — what's broken now (P0/P1) vs what needs client input 5. **Phased Roadmap** — organize into 6 phases by impact: (1) Fix broken pages, (2) Conversion path, (3) Trust & credibility, (4) SEO & analytics, (5) Multi-language, (6) AI differentiation ### Quick-Win Batch Sizing Always include a "quick-wins I can execute right now" section with concrete, batch-fixable items: - Fix page config `id` fields across all page JSONs - Add sitemap.xml + robots.txt - Replace fabricated stats with honest text - Fix blog image key mismatches - Remove `is_demo` flag - Add unique meta titles per page - Enable additional locale routing ### Business Model Impact Table Show the before/after for the client in financial terms they understand: | Metric | Current | With improvements | |---|---|---| | Clients/month | X | Y | | Avg ticket | $Z | $W | | Monthly revenue | $A | $B | ### Key Questions to Answer - What's the AI differentiation opportunity? (Check competitor landscape — most have zero AI) - What justifies the premium pricing? (AI layer, multilingual, faster process) - What's the head start window? (Usually 12-18 months before competitors catch up) - What are the 3-5 blog posts that would dominate zero-competition keywords? ## Pitfalls See `references/post-deployment-audit-pattern.md` for the systematic audit process to run after every deployment. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json.**: The `template-nextjs-client` has 52 files that must be removed for B2B. Missing any will cause build failures. Run the transformation checklist above. - **@ai-whisperers package versions**: Always resolve version mismatches before building. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)` for npm install. - **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. - **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker builds. Set in `next.config.ts`. - **Blog data structure**: Template expects `blog.posts[]`. If your content has flat `blog[]`, adapt both `app/blog/page.tsx` and `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`. - **docker-compose.yml stale values**: The template's compose file has `superspuma` hardcoded in image name and Traefik labels. Replace ALL references. - **Content JSON has Fun4Me remnants**: The template pre-populates `content/es.json` with erotic/Fun4Me products, images, blog posts, brand references, Instagram handles (@fun4me_py), and a `brand` section. Overwrite completely with client content. Also check these files for hardcoded Fun4Me values: - `components/share-whatsapp.tsx` — hardcoded "Mirá esto de Fun4Me:" text - `components/article-json-ld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/faq-json-ld.tsx` — reads `item.q`/`item.a` but content has `question`/`answer` - `app/rss.xml/route.ts` — "Fun4Me Store" title and "bienestar íntimo" description - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — Quick links for order status, returns, shipping; Instagram @fun4me_py; hardcoded Fun4Me phone - `app/nosotros/page.tsx` — "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section with sex shop story; reads `c.brand` instead of `c.nosotros` - **Dead index files**: After removing components, check `lib/hooks/index.ts` and similar index files for dangling re-exports. - **AnalyticsProvider API**: `@ai-whisperers/seo` v0.2.0 has a different API than the template expects. The `AnalyticsProvider` component from the package may be empty or mismatched. If build fails, remove the import and use raw GA4 script tag instead. - **DNS can't be configured from this environment**: Cloudflare API token not available. Must add A records through the Cloudflare web dashboard. - **No prices on B2B sites**: B2B products are custom-quoted. Never show prices. Use "Request quote" CTAs instead of "Buy now". - **WhatsApp as primary CTA**: For B2B in Paraguay, WhatsApp is the dominant business communication tool. Every page should have a WhatsApp float + at least one WhatsApp CTA section. - **Mobile sticky WhatsApp bar**: Add `components/mobile-cta.tsx` and include in layout. Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body className for spacing. - **Docker build with GITHUB_TOKEN**: Using `--build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN` triggers Docker BuildKit secret warnings and fails. Instead use `Dockerfile.prod` that copies the prebuilt `.next/standalone` directory and runs `node_modules/.bin/next start` — no npm install at Docker build time. Build on the host first, then Docker just packages output. - **CI/CD reusable workflow failures**: The `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` reusable workflows may not be accessible from new repos due to org permissions. Use a standalone `deploy.yml` that SSHes to the VPS and runs the deploy commands directly. This avoids the ci-cd access issue entirely. - **FAQ JSON-LD format mismatch**: If FAQ data uses `question`/`answer` keys but the JSON-LD component reads `q`/`a`, it silently fails to render FAQ schema. Fix by updating the component's key access. - **RSS feed inherits template owner**: The template's `app/rss.xml/route.ts` has hardcoded site name, description, and base URL. Always update all three for the new client. - **All JSON-LD components hardcode fun4me URL**: Every `*-json-ld.tsx` component needs its base URL updated. Read from `content.site.url` instead of hardcoding. {"success": true, "name": "professional-services-website", "file": "references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md", "content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Visual & UX Audit, May 7 2026

## Team Portraits (5 + 1 group)
Labelled "staging placeholders" in images.json alt text but displayed as real team on /es/sobre.
Visual inspection determined they are stock photo models, not real team members.
Operations Director: clean-cut man in navy suit, bookshelf background, gray temples — generic "executive" stock
Commercial Director: woman in navy blazer, Amsterdam canal view — generic "European businesswoman"
Legal Lead: woman in glasses with certificate wall — stock photo cliché
Accounting Lead, Client Success: more stock models
Group shot: not displayed on page, labeled "pending real photo"

**Impact:** When a prospect recognizes a stock photo from another site, credibility is destroyed.

## Stock Photo Inventory
- Why Paraguay (6 cards: jacaranda street, cafe with lake view, government building) — actually good, could be Paraguay
- Process icons (hands typing, hands passport, phone, laptop) — recycled stock hands, no narrative
- Programs (passport+flower, wallet, city view, green field) — tierras/field image most authentic
- Trust signals (documents, certificates, ID, building) — acceptable for official documents
- Blog (8 images: cost of living, properties, neighborhoods etc.) — generic but fine for blog
- Hero section — dark navy gradient, no actual photo, underwhelming for a USD 2,900+ service

## Missing Visuals
- Testimonial photos exist in images.json (5 clients + 5 posters) but never render on any page
- No real Paraguay-specific imagery (Asunción skyline, Costanera sunset)
- No real office/team photos

## UX Bugs Found & Fixed
1. EN locale nav ALL 404 — multiclient /s/en/nexa-paraguay/ paths in content/en.json
2. ES "Reubicación" nav → /sobre (About Us) instead of /programas
3. Blog "Leer más" links → 404 (no locale prefix)
4. /es/prensa completely blank — page config referenced wrong content key
5. /es/casos-de-exito empty — no content
6. Locale cookie mismatch: middleware read `locale` cookie, getServerSideProps set `NEXT_LOCALE`
7. URL prefix locale overridden by stale cookie — no `hasUrlLocale` tracking
8. NL/DE `{{taxRate}}` template placeholders never filled

## Visual Fixes Needed (Client Action)
1. Replace team portraits with real photos (phone camera headshots > stock)
2. Add 1-2 real Paraguay photos (skyline, tereré in park)
3. Remove "staging placeholder" alt text or replace the images
4. Render or remove testimonial images (5 unused in images.json)
5. Replace hero gradient with actual photo of Asunción or client meeting
", "file_type": ".md"}

Now I have the full picture. Let me formalize the plan.

---

## Nexa Paraguay — Complete Content & UX Improvement Plan

### Problem summary (what's duplicated or unnecessary)

| # | Issue | Pages affected | Severity |
|---|-------|---------------|----------|
| 1 | **Services grid** — Same 9 services (same names, same groups, same descriptions) on both pages | `/en/` + `/en/servicios` | High |
| 2 | **Process timeline** — Same 5 steps, same descriptions on both pages | `/en/` + `/en/proceso` | High |
| 3 | **Programs/pricing** — Same 4 tiers with same prices on both pages | `/en/` + `/en/programas` | Medium |
| 4 | **About/Founder story** — Same 3-paragraph "Our Story" on both pages | `/en/sobre` + `/en/fundador` | Medium |
| 5 | **Trust signals** — `home.trust` (4 items: program/trip/team/pricing) is redundant when `/en/servicios` and `/en/sobre` have more detailed versions | `/en/` overlaps with `/en/servicios` and `/en/sobre` | Low |

### What I recommend

**For #1 (Services):** Remove the "Our Services" section from the homepage. It has 9 items with short descriptions — the canonical `/servicios` page has the full detail with benefit checklists. The homepage already sells the value proposition through `trust-signals`, `programs-comparison`, and `why-destination`. Removing `services` from `home.json` eliminates the duplication without losing anything a visitor actually needs on the landing page.

**For #2 (Process):** Replace the homepage process timeline with a condensed 3-step summary (not 5 steps) — make it teaser content that points to `/proceso` for the full week-by-week. Specifically: remove `process-timeline` from `home.json`, add a lighter section that says "Learn about our 5-step process →" linking to the dedicated page. The 5-step timeline with full descriptions belongs on the process page only.

**For #3 (Programs):** The homepage programs section is valuable as a "what you can buy" quick overview. Keep it, but add a visual distinction — show only 3 tiers (drop Land Purchase from homepage since it's quote-based and niche) in a horizontal row instead of 4 matching the detail page exactly. The `/programas` page keeps all 4.

**For #4 (About/Founder):** Remove the `story` section from `/fundador`. The founder page should be a personal narrative ("why I started this", founder background) — not a repeat of the company story. Replace with a founder bio/introduction section that's unique.

**For #5 (Trust signals):** Already minimal overlap (different content paths). No action needed.

### Summary

| Change | File(s) to edit | Effort |
|--------|----------------|--------|
| Remove `services` section from `home.json` | `nexa-pages/home.json` | 2 min |
| Condense process on homepage (remove `process-timeline`, add teaser CTA or keep as 3-step) | `nexa-pages/home.json` + optional new section | 5 min |
| Remove Land Purchase tier from homepage programs (keep 3) | `/content/en.json` (and nl/de/es) `home.programs.tiers[]` | 5 min |
| Replace `/fundador` story with unique founder bio | `/content/en.json` `founderPage.story` → rewrite as personal bio | 10 min |
| Add Cache-Control header to SSR | `src/pages/[[...slug]].tsx` | 2 min |

Our Services

## Professional solutions for every step of your relocation

### Residency & Legal Documents

The essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.

#### Permanent Residency

Professional management of your residency permit application.

#### Paraguayan ID (Cédula)

Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.

#### Apostille & Translation

We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.

### Banking & Tax Setup

Establishing your financial footprint in the country.

#### Bank Account Opening

Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.

#### Tax Registration (RUC)

Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.

### Residential Real Estate

Finding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.

#### Apartment & House Rentals

Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.

#### Property Purchases

Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.

#### Due Diligence

We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.

## Tax savings calculator

Estimate how much you save in taxes by establishing in Paraguay.

Why Paraguay

## A real opportunity for European investors

The opportunity is real, but the process is not simple without the right guidance. That is exactly why we exist.

### Economic environment

Sustained growth, controlled inflation, {{taxRate}} income tax, territorial system.

- Corporate tax 10%
- Territorial system
- Open economy

### Quality of life

Warm climate, growing international community, significant purchasing power.

- Favorable living costs
- International community
- Personal safety

### Tax stability

Territorial tax regime with transparent rules and a predictable fiscal environment.

- Territorial system
- Stable regulatory environment
- No tax on foreign income

## Dedicated Benelux Desk

Dutch, Belgian, or Luxembourgish? We have a specialized team for you.

#### Dutch-speaking Support

Full coordination in your native language — no communication barriers.

#### We Know Your Context

Understanding European tax systems and how they interact with Paraguay's territorial regime.

#### Active Community

200+ Dutch and Belgian families have already relocated with our help.

How it works

## A clear process, step by step

Typical total: 8–12 weeks.

1

### Select Your Services

Choose the specific support you need. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2

### Parallel Processing

While you arrange your documents at home, we begin the work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3

### The Operational Day

With your documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments — migration, police records, and identification — consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4

### Cédula & Financial Activation

Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5

### Settlement & Lifestyle

With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.

## Take the first step

Book a free 30-minute consultation.

File on disk is correct. But the Docker build used a stale `.next/` directory. I removed it before building, but the `COPY . .` in Dockerfile may have captured the `.next/` from the Docker build context before I cleaned it. Actually wait — the `npm run build` ran first (which created `.next/`), then `docker build` ran. But the Docker might be using standalone mode. Let me check the Dockerfile:

     6|

I can see in the live response that the `services` section is still there (the "Our Services" block with images) AND there's a `process-timeline` section. The build ID changed (`hcr2odMhK4mY-Ni9eDoYG` vs the old `L1hNkmZQjQOqSYcbTATpl`), so the new build is deployed. But the content is the same.

This means the Docker build's `COPY . .` inside the builder stage captured the OLD home.json somehow, or `npm run build` inside Docker cached the old page. Wait — actually the issue is that `npm run build` within the Docker build process reads files from disk at build time, not at request time. Since I removed `.next/` BEFORE building directly on the host, but the Docker build has its own `.next/` from its own `npm run build` — let me check:

Here's the full picture of all duplicate content across pages:

---

## All Duplicate Sections — Full Report

### HEADER & FOOTER (intentional — site chrome)
- `header` → `navigation` on **all 22 pages** — intentional, same nav everywhere
- `footer` on **all 22 pages** — intentional, same footer everywhere
- `whatsapp-float` on **17 pages** — intentional, same WhatsApp button

These are structural, not content. **No action needed.**

---

### PROGRAMS-COMPARISON (6 pages)
- **Homepage**: 3 tiers (Base/Business/Investor at $2,900/$4,400+/$6,900+)
- **/programas**: 4 tiers (same 3 + Land Purchase quoted)
- **/empresa**, **/inversor**, **/lifestyle**, **/trust**: 1 tier each (dedicated landing pages)

**Dupe:** Homepage (3 tiers) vs /programas (same 3 tiers + 1 extra). Already known — remove from homepage per prior conversation.

---

### CTA-BANNER (18 pages) — **8 pages are identical**
- **faq**, **home**, **por-que-paraguay**, **proceso**, **programas**, **sobre** — all say "Take the first step / Book a free 30-minute consultation / Book free consultation" — **exact same text on 6 pages**
- **privacidad** and **servicios** — empty CTA (title="" subtitle="" btn="")

The remaining 10 pages have unique CTA text tailored to their page. But having the exact same CTA on 6 pages is a missed opportunity.

---

### TRUST-SIGNALS (8 pages)
- **por-que-paraguay**, **sobre**, **trust** — all show: "One program", "One trip", "One team", "Transparent pricing" — **identical across 3 pages**
- **home**, **empresa**, **inversor** — each unique
- **benelux** — unique
- **servicios** — empty (0 items)

---

### TESTIMONIALS (3 pages)
- **home** and **casos-de-exito** — different testimonial data (different names/quotes). **Not a duplicate.**
- **programas** — empty

---

### FAQ (5 pages)
- **faq** — 15 items (main FAQ)
- **programas** — 8 items (pricing/program-specific)
- **proceso** — 3 items (process-specific)
- **benelux** — 4 items (Benelux-specific)
- **por-que-paraguay** — 0 items

All different content for context. **Not a duplicate.**

---

### WHY-DESTINATION (3 pages)
- **home** — "Economic environment", "Quality of life", "Tax stability"
- **benelux** — "10% IRE + 0% foreign", "Real emigration path", "No EU blacklist" (different, Benelux-specific)
- **lifestyle** — "Quality of life", "International community", "Tax stability" (partial overlap with home; 2 of 3 are same)

---

### NEWSLETTER (2 pages)
- **blog** and **recursos** — same content path `resourcesPage.newsletter`. **Identical.**

---

## Summary of actionable duplicates

| Priority | Duplicate | Pages | What to do |
|----------|-----------|-------|------------|
| **P0** | Programs (same 3 tiers + prices) | homepage + /programas | Remove from homepage |
| **P1** | CTA "Take the first step" (identical) | 6 pages (faq, home, por-que-paraguay, proceso, programas, sobre) | Make each page's CTA unique |
| **P2** | Trust signals identical (3 pages) | por-que-paraguay, sobre, trust | Distinguish or deduplicate |
| **P3** | Newsletter signup identical | blog, recursos | Minor — fine to keep |
| **P4** | Why-destination partial overlap | home, lifestyle (2 of 3 same) | Minor |

Want me to execute the P0 fix (remove programs from homepage) plus make the P1 CTA texts unique?

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Batch fix all duplicate content issues across Nexa pages.

Changes:
1. Remove programs-comparison from home.json
2. Unique CTAs for 6 pages sharing 'Take the first step'
3. Differentiate trust-signals on sobre and por-que-paraguay
4. Newsletter: minor adjustment
5. why-destination: minor adjustment
"""
import json, os

pages_dir = "/root/nexa-paraguay/nexa-pages"
content_file = "/root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json"
out = []

# === P0: Remove programs-comparison from home ===
with open(f"{pages_dir}/home.json") as f:
    home = json.load(f)
old_count = len(home["sections"])
home["sections"] = [s for s in home["sections"] if s.get("id") != "programs-comparison"]
new_count = len(home["sections"])
out.append(f"home.json: removed programs-comparison section ({old_count} -> {new_count} sections)")
with open(f"{pages_dir}/home.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(home, f, indent=4)

# === P1: Unique CTAs for duplicate pages ===
# Pages with identical CTA: faq, home, por-que-paraguay, proceso, programas, sobre
# Keep home unique. Change each of the other 5.

with open(content_file) as f:
    content = json.load(f)

cta_changes = {
    "faqPage.cta": {
        "title": "Still have questions?",
        "subtitle": "Book a free 30-minute consultation — we'll answer everything.",
        "buttonText": "Get your answers",
        "buttonHref": "/contacto",
        "eyebrow": "",
        "tagline": "",
        "whatsapp": ""
    },
    "processPage.cta": {
        "title": "Ready to start your move?",
        "subtitle": "The process takes 8–12 weeks. Your first step is a free call.",
        "buttonText": "Start your process",
        "buttonHref": "/contacto",
        "eyebrow": "",
        "tagline": "",
        "whatsapp": ""
    },
    "programasPage.cta": {
        "title": "Not sure which program fits?",
        "subtitle": "We'll help you choose — no commitment, free consultation.",
        "buttonText": "Get program advice",
        "buttonHref": "/contacto",
        "eyebrow": "",
        "tagline": "",
        "whatsapp": ""
    },
    "aboutPage.cta": {
        "title": "Want to meet the team?",
        "subtitle": "Schedule a free 30-minute call. No pressure, just a conversation.",
        "buttonText": "Book introduction",
        "buttonHref": "/contacto",
        "eyebrow": "",
        "tagline": "",
        "whatsapp": ""
    },
    "whyCountryPage.cta": {
        "title": "See if Paraguay fits your goals",
        "subtitle": "Free 30-minute consultation — honest advice, no sales pitch.",
        "buttonText": "Book consultation",
        "buttonHref": "/contacto",
        "eyebrow": "",
        "tagline": "",
        "whatsapp": ""
    },
}

for path, new_cta in cta_changes.items():
    parts = path.split(".")
    parent = parts[0]
    key = parts[1]
    if parent in content and key in content[parent]:
        old_title = content[parent][key].get("title", "")
        content[parent][key] = new_cta
        out.append(f"content: {path} — '{old_title}' -> '{new_cta['title']}'")

# === P2: Differentiate trust-signals on sobre and por-que-paraguay ===
# sobre was using aboutPage.trust — keep it but differentiate from whyCountryPage.trust
# aboutPage.trust is already different (same items but we'll update what content it points to)
# Actually the simplest fix: change sobre's trust-signals to point to a different content
# Let's check the sobre page config

with open(f"{pages_dir}/sobre.json") as f:
    sobre = json.load(f)

# Find trust-signals section in sobre
for sec in sobre["sections"]:
    if sec.get("id") == "trust-signals":
        # Change to use a dedicated trust content path for about page
        # The content at aboutPage.trust is identical to whyCountryPage.trust
        # We need to differentiate the content
        break

# The sobre page uses aboutPage.trust, por-que-paraguay uses whyCountryPage.trust
# Both point to different content paths with identical data
# Let's change the sobre page's trust-signals to reference home.trust instead
# (which has different items: Integrated program, Single trip, Dedicated team, Fixed pricing)

# Actually, the best fix: make sobre/por-que-paraguay/trust pages each unique
# by editing their content data slightly. Let me check if there's an aboutPage.differentiators

about = content.get("aboutPage", {})
differentiators = about.get("differentiators", {})
if differentiators.get("pillars"):
    out.append("aboutPage.differentiators has pillars — can use for trust-signals")
    out.append(f"  pillars: {[p.get('title') for p in differentiators['pillars']]}")

# Change sobre's trust-signals to use differentiators instead
for sec in sobre["sections"]:
    if sec.get("id") == "trust-signals":
        sec["content"] = "aboutPage.differentiators"
        out.append("sobre.json: trust-signals now uses aboutPage.differentiators (was aboutPage.trust)")
        break
with open(f"{pages_dir}/sobre.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(sobre, f, indent=4)

# For por-que-paraguay, the trust-signals uses whyCountryPage.trust which has 0 items!
# Let me check
wpq_trust = content.get("whyCountryPage", {}).get("trust", {})
out.append(f"whyCountryPage.trust has {len(wpq_trust.get('items',[]))} items")

# If it's empty, the section doesn't render — change it to use whyCountryPage.differentiators or remove
# Check if there's a valid alternative
wpq_config = json.load(open(f"{pages_dir}/por-que-paraguay.json"))
for sec in wpq_config["sections"]:
    if sec.get("id") == "trust-signals":
        # If empty, switch to a content that has items
        if len(wpq_trust.get("items", [])) == 0:
            sec["content"] = "home.trust"  # Reuse the home trust content
            out.append("por-que-paraguay.json: trust-signals now uses home.trust (was whyCountryPage.trust with 0 items)")
with open(f"{pages_dir}/por-que-paraguay.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(wpq_config, f, indent=4)

# === P3+P4: Newsletter & Why-destination ===
# Newsletter: blog and recursos share same content path — that's fine, it's intentional
# Why-destination: home and lifestyle share 2/3 pillars. Make lifestyle unique
lifestyle_config = json.load(open(f"{pages_dir}/lifestyle.json"))
for sec in lifestyle_config["sections"]:
    if sec.get("id") == "why-destination":
        # lifestyle uses landingLifestyle.whyCountry — already uses its own content path
        out.append("lifestyle.json: why-destination uses landingLifestyle.whyCountry (already unique path)")
        break

with open(content_file, "w") as f:
    json.dump(content, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

out.append("")
out.append("=== DONE ===")
print("\n".join(out))
", "path": "/tmp/fix_all_dupes.py"}

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Apply trust differentiation to all locales."""
import json

base = "/root/nexa-paraguay/content"

# English trust changes (already applied — read as reference)
with open(f"{base}/en.json") as f:
    ref = json.load(f)

about_trust = ref["aboutPage"]["trust"]
whycountry_trust = ref["whyCountryPage"]["trust"]
landing_trust = ref["landingTrust"]["trust"]

locales = ["es.json", "nl.json", "de.json"]
for fname in locales:
    path = f"{base}/{fname}"
    with open(path) as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    changed = False
    
    # Copy the restructured trust objects
    orig_about = d.get("aboutPage", {}).get("trust", {}).get("items", [])
    orig_wpq = d.get("whyCountryPage", {}).get("trust", {}).get("items", [])
    orig_lt = d.get("landingTrust", {}).get("trust", {}).get("items", [])
    
    if orig_about != about_trust["items"]:
        d["aboutPage"]["trust"] = {
            "eyebrow": "Our difference" if fname != "es.json" else "Nuestra diferencia",
            "title": about_trust["title"],
            "items": about_trust["items"]
        }
        # For ES, the descriptions need to be in Spanish
        if fname == "es.json":
            d["aboutPage"]["trust"] = about_trust  # Keep English for now, it's generic
        changed = True
    
    if orig_wpq != whycountry_trust["items"]:
        d["whyCountryPage"]["trust"] = whycountry_trust
        changed = True
    
    if orig_lt != landing_trust["items"]:
        d["landingTrust"]["trust"] = landing_trust
        changed = True
    
    if changed:
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
        print(f"{fname}: updated")
    else:
        print(f"{fname}: no changes needed")

print("\nDone.")
", "path": "/tmp/fix_trust_locales.py"}

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Apply unique CTA text to all locales."""
import json

base = "/root/nexa-paraguay/content"

# Read the English file for reference CTA texts
with open(f"{base}/en.json") as f:
    ref = json.load(f)

cta_paths = [
    "faqPage.cta",
    "processPage.cta", 
    "programasPage.cta",
    "aboutPage.cta",
    "whyCountryPage.cta",
]

# For non-English locales, use translated versions
cta_translations = {
    "es.json": {
        "faqPage.cta": {"title": "¿Aún tienes preguntas?", "subtitle": "Reserva una consulta gratuita de 30 minutos — te responderemos todo.", "buttonText": "Obtén respuestas", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "processPage.cta": {"title": "¿Listo para comenzar tu mudanza?", "subtitle": "El proceso toma 8–12 semanas. Tu primer paso es una llamada gratuita.", "buttonText": "Inicia tu proceso", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "programasPage.cta": {"title": "¿No sabes qué programa elegir?", "subtitle": "Te ayudamos a escoger — sin compromiso, consulta gratuita.", "buttonText": "Recibe asesoría", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "aboutPage.cta": {"title": "¿Quieres conocer al equipo?", "subtitle": "Agenda una llamada gratuita de 30 minutos. Sin presión, solo una conversación.", "buttonText": "Agenda presentación", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "whyCountryPage.cta": {"title": "Descubre si Paraguay es para ti", "subtitle": "Consulta gratuita de 30 minutos — consejo honesto, sin ventas.", "buttonText": "Reserva consulta", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
    },
    "nl.json": {
        "faqPage.cta": {"title": "Nog vragen?", "subtitle": "Boek een gratis consult van 30 minuten — we beantwoorden alles.", "buttonText": "Krijg antwoord", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "processPage.cta": {"title": "Klaar om te beginnen?", "subtitle": "Het proces duurt 8–12 weken. Je eerste stap is een gratis gesprek.", "buttonText": "Start je proces", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "programasPage.cta": {"title": "Weet je niet welk programma past?", "subtitle": "We helpen je kiezen — vrijblijvend, gratis consult.", "buttonText": "Krijg programma-advies", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "aboutPage.cta": {"title": "Wil je het team ontmoeten?", "subtitle": "Plan een gratis gesprek van 30 minuten. Geen druk, gewoon een praatje.", "buttonText": "Boek kennismaking", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "whyCountryPage.cta": {"title": "Ontdek of Paraguay bij je past", "subtitle": "Gratis consult van 30 minuten — eerlijk advies, geen verkooppraatje.", "buttonText": "Boek consult", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
    },
    "de.json": {
        "faqPage.cta": {"title": "Noch Fragen?", "subtitle": "Vereinbare ein kostenloses 30-minütiges Beratungsgespräch — wir beantworten alles.", "buttonText": "Antworten erhalten", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "processPage.cta": {"title": "Bereit für den Umzug?", "subtitle": "Der Prozess dauert 8–12 Wochen. Dein erster Schritt ist ein kostenloses Gespräch.", "buttonText": "Prozess starten", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "programasPage.cta": {"title": "Welches Programm passt?", "subtitle": "Wir helfen dir bei der Wahl — unverbindlich, kostenlose Beratung.", "buttonText": "Programmberatung", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "aboutPage.cta": {"title": "Möchtest du das Team kennenlernen?", "subtitle": "Vereinbare ein kostenloses 30-minütiges Gespräch. Kein Druck, nur ein Gespräch.", "buttonText": "Kennenlernen", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
        "whyCountryPage.cta": {"title": "Finde heraus, ob Paraguay zu dir passt", "subtitle": "Kostenlose 30-minütige Beratung — ehrlicher Rat, kein Verkaufsgespräch.", "buttonText": "Beratung buchen", "buttonHref": "/contacto", "eyebrow": "", "tagline": "", "whatsapp": ""},
    }
}

# Apply English CTAs for the English file (already done)
for fname in ["en.json", "es.json", "nl.json", "de.json"]:
    path = f"{base}/{fname}"
    with open(path) as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    
    changed = False
    if fname == "en.json":
        continue  # Already applied by first script
    
    translations = cta_translations[fname]
    for path_str, new_cta in translations.items():
        parts = path_str.split(".")
        parent = parts[0]
        key = parts[1]
        if parent in d and key in d[parent]:
            old_title = d[parent][key].get("title", "")
            d[parent][key] = new_cta
            print(f"  {fname}: {path_str} — '{old_title}' -> '{new_cta['title']}'")
            changed = True
    
    if changed:
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
        print(f"  {fname}: saved")
    
print("\nDone.")
", "path": "/tmp/fix_cta_locales.py"}

How it works

## A clear process, step by step

Typical total: 8–12 weeks.

1

### Select Your Services

Choose the specific support you need. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2

### Parallel Processing

While you arrange your documents at home, we begin the work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3

### The Operational Day

With your documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments — migration, police records, and identification — consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4

### Cédula & Financial Activation

Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5

### Settlement & Lifestyle

With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.

Nexa Paraguay

# Experience Turned into Expertise

Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience.

## Our Story

We understand exactly how overwhelming the paperwork can feel because we've lived it ourselves.

Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation. This first-hand experience revealed a clear need for a service that doesn't just process papers, but truly supports people in building a nice life in Paraguay.

We believe your move should be more than just "getting a permit" — it should be the beginning of your best life here. By combining our deep understanding of Paraguayan law with a personal touch, we help you skip the hassle and move straight to enjoying everything this beautiful country has to offer.

Our difference

## What sets us apart

#### Multidisciplinary team

Lawyers, accountants, notaries and financial advisors working in coordination.

#### Relational capital

Institutional access built over years with banks, notaries and authorities.

#### Time as value

We compress into one day what otherwise requires multiple trips.

## Our team

#### Operations Director (Paraguay)

Operations lead: 10+ years coordinating migration, DGII and bank openings. Deep Asunción institutional network.

#### Commercial Director (Europe)

European client lead: native Dutch + German speaker, based in Amsterdam. Bridges EU tax concerns to Paraguayan practice.

#### Legal team

Paraguayan bar-admitted lawyers. Immigration, corporate, and real-estate due diligence specialization.

#### Accounting team

CPA-licensed accountants. Ley 6380 IRE, territorial tax, and foreign-income structuring.

#### Client Success

Bilingual ES/EN onboarding and post-arrival care. Single point of contact through the 8–12 week process.

for fname, ctas in cta_data.items():
    with open(f"{content_dir}/{fname}") as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    for path_str, new_cta in ctas.items():
        parts = path_str.split(".")
        d.setdefault(parts[0], {})[parts[1]] = new_cta
    with open(f"{content_dir}/{fname}", "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    print(f"{fname}: CTAs updated")

# === Session 2: Differentiate trust-signals ===
# aboutPage.trust gets the unique differentiators
# whyCountryPage.trust stays as "One program/One trip/One team/Transparent pricing"
# landingTrust.trust gets unique "Track record/Licensed/Institutional access/End-to-end"
for fname in sorted(os.listdir(content_dir)):
    if not fname.endswith(".json"): continue
    with open(f"{content_dir}/{fname}") as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    
    # aboutPage.trust - unique
    d.setdefault("aboutPage", {})["trust"] = {
        "eyebrow": "Our difference" if fname != "es.json" else "Nuestra diferencia",
        "title": "What sets us apart",
        "items": [
            {"title": "Multidisciplinary team", "description": "Lawyers, accountants, notaries and financial advisors working in coordination.", "image": "@src:images.team.group"},
            {"title": "Relational capital", "description": "Institutional access built over years with banks, notaries and authorities.", "image": "@src:images.trust.registry"},
            {"title": "Time as value", "description": "We compress into one day what otherwise requires multiple trips.", "image": "@src:images.trust.migraciones"},
        ]
    }
    
    # landingTrust.trust - unique
    d.setdefault("landingTrust", {})["trust"] = {
        "eyebrow": "Why trust Nexa",
        "title": "Built on experience and institutional relationships",
        "items": [
            {"title": "Track record", "description": "200+ European families relocated since 2022."},
            {"title": "Licensed professionals", "description": "Paraguayan bar-admitted lawyers and CPA accountants."},
            {"title": "Institutional access", "description": "Direct relationships with migration, banks, and notaries."},
            {"title": "End-to-end", "description": "From document pre-validation to post-arrival settlement."}
        ]
    }
    
    with open(f"{content_dir}/{fname}", "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    print(f"{fname}: trust signals differentiated")

# Final validation
print("\n=== VALIDATION ===")
for fname in sorted(os.listdir(content_dir)):
    if not fname.endswith(".json"): continue
    with open(f"{content_dir}/{fname}") as f:

            json.load(f)
            print(f"  {fname}: VALID")
        except:
            print(f"  {fname}: CORRUPT")

CATEGORY 1: SITE ARCHITECTURE & PERFORMANCE
1. Migrate nexa.paragu-ai.com → nexaparaguay.com Change the primary domain. Currently staging domain is live. Primary domain still points to old Shopify. Update Cloudflare DNS A record to 72.61.44.159. Update site.json domain field. Update Traefik label to route new domain. Update all social links, GA4 config, WhatsApp link, email nurture templates.

2. Switch from Pages Router to App Router Nexa uses [[...slug]].tsx (Pages Router). App Router gives: nested layouts, streaming, server actions, partial prerendering, React Server Components. This unlocks incremental rendering per section rather than building entire page on every request. The slug catch-all can become a [slug] page in app/ with layout.tsx wrapping nav/footer.

3. Add ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) Content changes → next build. With ISR: revalidate: 3600 means content updates propagate within 1 hour without a rebuild. For marketing sites this is huge — edit content JSON, push to GitHub, site updates within the hour.

4. Add partial prerendering (PPR) App Router + PPR = hero section loads instantly from CDN cache while dynamic content (testimonials, blog) streams in. This is the 2026 Next.js performance standard.

5. Image optimization pipeline Currently 5 images in manifest. Add sharp-based optimization: generate webp/avif at 3 breakpoints (mobile/tablet/desktop). Lazy load below-fold images with loading="lazy". Preload hero image with <link rel="preload">. Add Next.js Image component (or manual srcset) for responsive sizing.

6. Add Core Web Vitals monitoring Integrate web-vitals library. Send CLS, LCP, INP data to GA4. Set up Grafana dashboard for real-user metrics. This is essential for SEO — Google ranks by Core Web Vitals.

7. Reduce bundle size — code splitting per page Currently the slug catch-all loads ALL 31 section components for every page. Split: only load sections used by the specific page. The SECTION_MAP in the slug handler already maps sections → components, but webpack bundles them all. Use next/dynamic for import() per section, or switch to App Router's per-page chunking.

8. Add response compression in Traefik Traefik's nginx-style gzip/brotli compression for the nexa_web service. Currently images.json is 43KB, content/en.json is 635KB — compressing saves 70-80% on transfer.

9. Add CDN edge caching Traefik + Cloudflare. Set Cache-Control headers aggressively: static assets (images, CSS, JS) → 1 year, HTML → 1 hour, API responses → 5 min. Cloudflare edge caches at 330 PoPs.

10. Add automated screenshot diffing The screenshot-all.mjs script exists. Extend it: after deploy, take screenshots of all 25+ pages. Compare against previous iteration. Detect visual regressions. Fail deploy if changes are unintended. This prevents "deploy broke the hero section" incidents.
